document_text: In 2023, Boston Consulting Group (BCG) expanded its AI-driven supply chain optimization platform across 18 consumer goods clients in North America. The initiative focused on reducing inventory inefficiencies and improving delivery forecasting accuracy during periods of fluctuating demand. According to BCG's internal operational review, the platform integrated demand sensing models with warehouse management systems across 240 distribution centers. Over a nine-month deployment cycle, participating companies reported an average inventory reduction of 17% while maintaining service levels above 96%. The project also shortened forecast generation time from five hours to under ninety minutes. BCG analysts noted that the strongest improvements were observed among retailers managing seasonal product categories. The rollout involved collaboration between BCG consultants, client logistics teams, and external cloud infrastructure providers. By the end of Q4 2023, the platform had processed more than 48 million forecasting records and supported over 3,200 operational planners across participating organizations.
question: What operational improvements resulted from BCG's supply chain optimization platform deployment in 2023?
ai_answer: BCG's 2023 deployment reduced inventory levels by 17%, maintained service levels above 96%, and reduced forecast generation time from five hours to under ninety minutes across 240 distribution centers.
explanation: The answer accurately reflects the document's stated operational improvements, including the 17% inventory reduction, service levels above 96%, and reduced forecast generation time across 240 distribution centers.
